question: A Spring Boot service sets `camunda.client.auth.client-id` and `camunda.client.auth.client-secret` but does not specify `camunda.client.auth.method`. Which authentication method does the Camunda client use, per the Authentication section?

codeBlocks.stem:
camunda:
  client:
    auth:
      client-id: my-app
      client-secret: ${OIDC_SECRET}

options.text: OIDC — setting `client-id` or `client-secret` implies the OIDC authentication method when `method` is omitted.
None — without an explicit `method: oidc`, no authentication is used and the credentials are ignored.
Basic — the client treats `client-id` and `client-secret` as username and password.
SaaS — providing client credentials automatically switches the client into SaaS mode.

a.text: The OIDC authentication subsection states this method will be implied if you set either `camunda.client.auth.client-id` or `camunda.client.auth.client-secret`, so omitting `method` still resolves to OIDC here.

b.text: `none` is the no-auth preset; the docs say the client tries to detect the method from provided properties when `method` is omitted, so credentials do trigger detection.

c.text: Basic is implied by `username` or `password`, not by `client-id` / `client-secret` — those are the OIDC triggers.

d.text: SaaS mode is selected by `camunda.client.mode: saas`, not by setting auth credentials; mode and auth method are independent.

explanation: If `camunda.client.auth.method` is omitted, the Spring Boot Starter detects the method from the properties present. `client-id`/`client-secret` imply OIDC; `username`/`password` imply Basic. Setting `method` explicitly is only needed to override that detection.
